title: Dassam Falls
tagline: Dassam Falls — heritage city of Jharkhand

overview.short: The Dassam Falls (also known as Dassam Ghagh) is a waterfall located near Taimara village in the Bundu subdivision of Ranchi district in the Indian state of Jharkhand.
overview.description: The Dassam Falls (also known as Dassam Ghagh) is a waterfall located near Taimara village in the Bundu subdivision of Ranchi district in the Indian state of Jharkhand. The Falls are 144 feet in height, formed by the waterflow of the Kanchi River.

topPlaces.name: Sodag
topPlaces.description: Sodag is a small Village/hamlet in Khunti Block in Khunti District of Jharkhand State, India. It comes under Ganeor Panchayath.

Maranghada is a village in the Khunti CD block in the Khunti Sadar subdivision of the Khunti district in the Indian state of Jharkhand.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Dassam Falls?
faq.a: The best time to visit Dassam Falls is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Dassam Falls?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Birsa Munda Airport, Ranchi (24 km) and the nearest railway station is Ranchi Junction (29 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Dassam Falls?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Dassam Falls at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Dassam Falls?
faq.a: Stays in Dassam Falls start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Dassam Falls?
faq.a: The top attractions are Sodag, Ganeor, Maranghada.
